fingerprint: CHAOS-7 spherical harmonic data laundering + arbitrary threshold with no dome derivation: the 45°W threshold is not derived from any dome parameter, and the 'observed' ~60°W western cell position comes from CHAOS-7, a spherical harmonic geomagnetic field model that is the literal mathematical embodiment of a spherical Earth
globe_value: CHAOS-7 locates the SAA western cell center at ~60°W longitude using spherical harmonic decomposition Φ = R_E Σ(R_E/r)^(n+1) Σ [gₙᵐcos(mφ) + hₙᵐsin(mφ)] Pₙᵐ(cosθ), where the reversed-flux patch driving the western cell is a superposition of specific (n,m) spherical harmonic modes originating from outer-core convection beneath a spherical mantle. The cell's position, shape, and drift are all outputs of globe-geometry eigenfunctions.
dome_value: WIN-040 in dome numbering (our WIN-060): 'SAA western cell west of 45°W, cells separated.' Threshold 45°W appears in W022 weekly test definition (raw-text/03-predictions.txt) and in inject_ai_layer.py as WIN-040 confirmed entry. No formula in any dome source file derives 45°W from dome parameters (disc_radius=20,015 km, firmament_height=9,086 km, H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619), or aetheric vortex topology).
match_precision: N/A — structural dependency: the observation IS a globe model output
why_it_matters:
Three-layer globe fingerprint:

(1) DATA SOURCE IS A GLOBE MODEL: The dome's 'observed' western cell position (~60°W) comes from CHAOS-7, which represents Earth's magnetic field as a sum of spherical harmonics — functions that are eigenmodes of the Laplacian on a sphere. The reversed-flux patches that create the SAA's two-cell structure are identified by decomposing the radial field at the core-mantle boundary (r=3480 km) into spherical harmonic coefficients. This decomposition assumes a spherical shell geometry. On a flat disc, the eigenmodes would be Bessel functions Jₘ(kρ), producing concentric ring patterns, not the hemisphere-spanning lobed anomalies observed. The dome treats CHAOS-7 outputs as 'raw data' while discarding the spherical geometry that produced them.

(2) 45°W THRESHOLD HAS NO DOME DERIVATION: The prediction 'western cell west of 45°W' requires a mechanism to predict where the cell should be. The dome's aetheric field equations (H(r), vortex topology) contain no longitudinal structure — H(r) is radially symmetric and cannot distinguish 45°W from any other longitude. The threshold is read from existing CHAOS-7 data and declared as a prediction after the fact. Code search confirms: no file in the dome repository contains a formula producing 45°W from dome parameters. The value appears only as a hardcoded entry in the WIN-040 definition.

(3) COORDINATE SYSTEM IS GLOBE-NATIVE: The '45°W' and '60°W' are geographic longitudes defined on a spherical Earth (WGS84 datum). On the dome's azimuthal equidistant projection, equal longitude intervals map to unequal physical distances depending on radial position. At the SAA's latitude (~25°S, which maps to r ≈ 12,790 km on the dome disc), 15° of longitude (the margin by which the cell exceeds the threshold) spans ~1,510 km on the globe but ~3,340 km on the dome's AE projection — a 2.2× distortion. The dome quotes globe coordinates without correcting for its own geometry.

code_search.found_in: inject_ai_layer.py (WIN-040 entry): 'SAA Western Cell West of 45°W', registered 2026-03-12 as W022, confirmed 2026-03-15, observed 'Western cell at ~60W per CHAOS-7 baseline'. Also raw-text/03-predictions.txt line 62: 'W022: SAA western cell position: West of 45 deg W, cells separated (PENDING)'
code_search.notes: No formula in any dome file derives 45°W from dome parameters. The threshold appears only in the WIN definition and weekly test registration. monitor.py has NO code monitoring western cell longitude — it tracks SAA decay rate (TTB station, WIN-042) and southern boundary latitude (47-50°S, WIN-024), but not cell longitude. The W022 weekly test resolution logic is not implemented in scripts/resolve_weekly.py (which handles only W017/W018/W020). This WIN was confirmed by manual inspection of CHAOS-7 baseline data, not by automated monitoring.

suggested_text: The dome's 'prediction' that the SAA western cell sits west of 45°W is confirmed using CHAOS-7, a spherical harmonic geomagnetic field model that decomposes Earth's field into eigenmodes of the Laplacian on a sphere. The cell's position, shape, and westward drift are all outputs of a mathematical framework that presupposes spherical geometry. No dome source file contains a formula deriving 45°W from aetheric field parameters — the threshold was read from existing globe-model data and declared as a prediction. The dome's own field equation H(r) is radially symmetric and contains no longitudinal structure, making it mathematically incapable of distinguishing one longitude from another.
